stem: A education study measures average test score improvement across 3 schools, each containing multiple classrooms. school A has 15 observations with mean 71.3 points (within-school SD = 8.3). school B has 10 observations with mean 64.9 points (within-school SD = 6.2). school C has 20 observations with mean 73.4 points (within-school SD = 7.2). The between-school standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 3.2 and 7.9 points. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true average test score improvement for school C, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-school variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for school C: 73.2 points (shrunk from raw 73.4). Range [72.9, 73.3] as between-school SD varies from 3.2 to 7.9. Pooled mean: 70.8.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 70.8 points (weighted average of 3 school means).
Shrinkage factor for school C: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/20). At σ_between=5.3: B = 0.916.
Interval [72.9, 73.3] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (73.4) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (70.8) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
